_about: Documented code patches applied to the code binary (everything that is not translated data): render-path detours and their cave bodies, decoder hooks for the briefing system, and two gameplay tweaks. The builder asserts old_hex before writing new_hex. The autoload/font relocation words (module params, font pointer, heap floor) are computed by utils/arm9_layout.py, not listed here. _paved_ref_allowlist maps JP-image literal offsets that still reference paved donor strings to proof they are dead readers (compiled-out printf 0x020A3ECC arg pools); gate patch_literal_safety enforces it.

entries.why: 12px glyphs overflow the 10-tile-per-row name field of the 8px UI; the copy helper is detoured to a cave that clamps the tile-row budget so a wide name cannot alias the neighbouring name's tiles (ghost fragments).
carried-unit names longer than the 6-cell squad field left stale glyph cells behind; detour blanks the cells beyond the field width.

the shared 12px glyph plot rasterizer (0x2012FE4) has a 2-row tile context with stride8=13 whose row1[col0] aliases row0[col13]; writes crossing the 13-tile row boundary wrap around and erase the lower strip of the row's first glyphs. The prologue is detoured to a cave that early-returns such writes for the audited list surfaces; every other draw replays the stock prologue byte-identically.

the fixed 8x16 UI font has no CJK glyphs; the glyph-blit start is detoured to a trampoline that renders glyph slots >= 2196 from the relocated 12x12 atlas with a 12px advance, making every 8x16-font surface CJK-capable.

multi-line 12px caption lines end past the 80px strip the 8px engine allocates; the cell-pad loop is stopped at the strip boundary so captions keep the original tile budget (no ghost echo right of the box).

mission-briefing records carry inline pointers to full-length text in the briefing blob bank; the decoder entry is detoured to resolve those pointers (and lift the glyph cap) when the source lies in the briefing region.
companion to the entry hook: restores the decoder's cursor to the briefing record end so the interactive briefing viewer walks records correctly.
second detour of the BG tile-copy helper (info-panel context) into the clamp cave; same over-advance fix as 0x12C4A for another copy path.
the engine-A copy helper gets the same row-budget clamp via its own cave, removing stray glyph fragments above the info boxes.
menu text styles use a 3-colour LUT that maps 2bpp ink value 3 to transparent; CJK glyphs are drawn with ink 3 only, so menus rendered them blank. Detour patches LUT[3]=LUT[2] for such styles.
one store in the OBJ-text plotter wrote the wrong register, keeping ink-value-3 pixels transparent on the aptitude labels; storing r1 renders them.
the widened 112px speaker-name surface extends to x=128; moving the WIN1 right edge to x=133 keeps the expanded frame under the same translucent green treatment instead of exposing a brighter strip.

entries.what: dialogue nameplate frame draw wrapper call A
entries.why: preserve the original frame-resource draw, then extend both nameplate tilemap rows through screen tile x=16 via the scoped wrapper at 0x12D680.
save two additional registers so the local stack frame can grow by 0x100 bytes while keeping the original caller state balanced.
reserve enough scratch space for a 14x2-tile speaker-name surface and the unchanged dialogue-body surface.
the stock height immediate remains two tiles for the expanded surface; setting the descriptor flag bit separately keeps the established 12x12 CJK render path.
a 14-tile (112px) surface holds nine 12px glyph cells without changing the text origin or glyph advance.

entries.what: dialogue body scratch-pointer helper call A
entries.why: Thumb-1 cannot encode sp+0x460 in one instruction, so the shared helper at 0x12D6C0 computes the body buffer after the enlarged name surface and restores r1=0 for memset.

entries.what: dialogue body scratch-pointer helper call B
entries.why: reuse the same sp+0x460 calculation when constructing the dialogue-body descriptor.
start body tiles after all 28 name-surface tiles so the two surfaces cannot overwrite each other.
release the enlarged final stack-allocation chunk before returning.
restore the two additional registers saved by the expanded prologue.
clear all 14x2x32 bytes of the widened name surface before rendering.
track the original caller argument after the local frame grows by 0x100 bytes.
construct a 14-tile-wide OBJ surface matching the enlarged scratch bitmap.

entries.what: dialogue body OBJ tile base 800->808 (creation path)
entries.why: keep the creation-time body tile base aligned with the 28-tile name surface.

entries.what: dialogue nameplate frame draw wrapper call B
entries.why: route the second frame-construction path through the same scoped x=1..16 tilemap wrapper.

entries.what: dialogue nameplate initialized blend-window right edge 101->133
entries.why: keep the initialized WIN1 range consistent with the widened nameplate before the first redraw.

entries.what: dialogue nameplate frame and body-pointer helpers
entries.why: dead bytes from the relocated original atlas host a 64-byte wrapper that preserves the stock frame draw and expands its two tilemap rows to left border + 14 green interior tiles + right border, followed by a 14-byte sp+0x460 body-buffer helper.

the speaker plate is renderA-direct (its 0x2013C00 call is not detoured through the penY+3 trampoline cave), so the 0x2BCA6 style fix left the plate's own penY=0 and 12px ink sat rows 0..11 where the JP 8x16 ink sat rows 2..14, touching the plate's top border; movs r2,#3 drops only this plate's baseline 3px (11+3=14 < 16, cannot clip) to the JP ink rows. Dialogue-body text uses different call sites and is untouched.
gameplay tweak: every stage transition gated on a free-battle (recon) count required 3-4 battles; each `cmp rN,#count ; bne` becomes `cmp rN,#1 ; blo` so a single free battle unlocks the next stage.
gameplay tweak: every stage transition gated on a free-battle (recon) count required 3-4 battles; each `cmp rN,#count ; bne` becomes `cmp rN,#1 ; blo` so a single free battle unlocks the next stage.

the unit/character list detail panel is an empty template until a unit is inspected; the detour populates the highlighted entry's panel while browsing; body relocated with the 0x1B3E22 cave (see the relocated-cave entry).
companion detour that refreshes the portrait/detail state when the roster cursor moves, so the auto-populated panel tracks navigation.

entries.what: 编成 detail-plate name render width 10 -> 11 tiles
entries.why: the pilot-name row of the 编成/一览 detail plate is OBJ text: the redraw callback renders the char-DB name via 0x2014470(mode 1, width r1 in 8px tiles, tile base 0x83, pal 15).  The JP design width is 10 tiles = 80px (the JP max name is exactly 80px), which mid-glyph-clips 7-cell (84px) translated names (艾帕·西纳普斯 lost 斯's right half).  11 tiles = 88px fits every <=84px name.  Must match the widget create width at 0x54BE0 (same value feeds the scratch stride and the commit chunker; a mismatch interleaves the tile columns).

entries.what: 编成 detail-plate name widget width 10 -> 11 tiles
entries.why: companion of 0x5487E: the page-open widget create (0x2014358: width r2, tile 0x83, x=64 y=8) allocates the OBJ sprite arrangement and tile block for the plate name row.  Width 11 makes the arranger emit 32+32+16+8px sprites per half-row (x=64..152) and the commit chunker copy 4+4+2+1 tiles into OBJ tiles 0x83..0x98 — 0x97/0x98 verified free on every OAM state of this page (the next allocation starts at 0x0C8-band and the portrait stops at 0x52).  Proven live: 艾帕·西纳普斯 renders complete, frame diff vs the 80px build is exactly the 斯-completion pixels.

entries.what: 配属 ID/ability BG char-tile partition
entries.why: the three left ID-command titles share one sequential engine-B char-tile bank starting at 0x263; at the registered 64px title budget each draw can consume r7=12 tiles per 8px row, so 3 titles x 2 rows reserve through tile 0x2AA.  The JP right-ability bank started at 0x29F and therefore overwrote tiles 0x29F..0x2A2 in the third title's lower row, producing wrong/missing glyph strokes.  Starting abilities at the exact non-overlap boundary 0x2AB leaves their own worst-case 72-tile reservation ending at 0x2F2, below the next 0x300 bank.

the battle-bark formatter consumed the high byte of a 2-byte glyph token after a 00 control marker (reading it as a control type), garbling the line; veneer to a cave that re-reads it as a normal token.
the ability learn-level getter is rebuilt to call a bounds-check helper cave so out-of-range learn slots cannot index past the table.

entries.what: 12x12-on-8x16 trampoline cave (+3px baseline anchor; slot-conditional advance)
entries.why: code cave in the dead in-image 12x12 atlas (the live atlas is relocated to RAM): renders glyph slots >= 2196 from the relocated atlas and advances the pen 12px; called from the 8x16 blit injection at 0x131D8. The atlas call is routed through a penY wrapper (cave tail +0xAC) that draws 12x12 glyphs at ctx.penY+3 and restores penY after: renderB ink sits on rows 2..14 of the 16px line box while the 12x12 cell inks rows 0..11, so an unbiased blit floats 3px above the renderB baseline (user-visible on every trampoline list row); +3 bottom-aligns ink (11+3=14) and cannot clip (14 < 16). renderA/stage surfaces are unaffected (their 0x2013108 call at 0x1323E is not detoured). Advance-select extension (cave tail +0xC2, @0x11A362): the pen adjustment after the atlas blit is slot-conditional — narrow-paren slots 4156 '(' / 4253 ')' get -2 (net 6px with the loop's +8), Latin burst-letter slots 4222 'S' / 4223 'E' / 4214 'D' get 0 (net 8px), every other atlas slot keeps +4 (net 12px).  r7 carries the delta across the blit (callee-saved by 0x2013108's push; the jump-back target 0x201320d pops the caller's r7), so battle plates / roster / ID pages / all trampoline pools render JP-shaped narrow parens and Latin-pitch SEED letters.  renderA-direct surfaces (stage text, the 0x2BCA6 speaker plate) are untouched (their 0x2013108 call is not detoured).

entries.what: engine-A tile-copy clamp cave (+ in-battle SPECIAL-description widen, col-3-scoped)
entries.why: row-budget clamp body for the engine-A copy helper (entered ONLY through the 0x1B3F0C cave chain, which preloads r0=ctx[0x12]+7 (copy width) and r1=ctx[0x5c] (dest VRAM) before the tail-jump — the leading reloads were dropped 2026-07-21 to make room; the sole reference to 0x0211C1FD is the 0x1B3F68 literal). Base behavior: OBJ-VRAM copies (ctx[0x5c]>>0x14==0x68) with tile-row>=0xa and width>=0x58 clamp to 0x50 (80px). The 2026-07-20 in-battle 情報 SPECIAL DEFENSE/ABILITY description widen (odd rows to 0xD0=208px, the box width) is now additionally gated on ctx[4]==3 (dest tile column 3, x=24): a py-desmume exec-hook trace of the OBJ cave proved the SPECIAL description rows are the ONLY col-3 odd-row copies (rows 0xb..0x15, widths ~0xD7-0xDB), while the in-battle ID COMMAND page performs FULL-STRIP compose copies at col 1 on the same odd rows 0xd..0x15 (widths 0xD7-0xEB spanning all three 80px panels): the unscoped odd-row widen let those strip copies run 208px and paved the neighbour panels with stale compose content — the '战场情报 ID command garbage' regression. col!=3 (or even-row) copies are restored to the pre-widen 0x50 clamp, byte-provably identical to the pre-regression behavior; col-3 odd rows keep min(width,0xD0) so the SPECIAL descriptions stay unclipped. Same 38-byte span (0x11C1FC..0x11C221, dead in-image atlas), disjoint from the row-stride clip (0x11C448).

entries.what: tile-copy clamp + roster auto-populate + special-box exemption caves (relocated into the dead in-image atlas)
entries.why: one combined cave: [+0x00] the 0x12C4A row-budget clamp body (map 0x0620F000, affinity/list contexts; tile_x==3 and tile_x==8&&tile_y>=5 branch into the exemption block; its shared return at +0x52 now tail-branches to the exact 配属-unit-name partition guard at 0x11C4B0), [+0x5A] the 0x4A340 roster browse auto-populate body (scratch byte +0xDA, roster-nav scratch +0xDB, both init 0xFF), [+0xDE] the special-box row exemption block (rows 0xC/0xE/0x10/0x14/0x16 of the SPECIAL DEFENSE/ABILITY boxes cap r7 at 0x1A, tile_x==8 path caps at 0x0B, other rows fall back into the clamp chain at +0x14). Relocated here from the 0x1B3E22/0x1B4040 dev-string spans: that band is NOT fully dead - it holds the live-referenced OBJ-text number-format strings 'D4' @0x021B3E90 and '/D4' @0x021B3E94 (literal pools 0x23640/0x23648/0x240A8/0x240B0) that the in-battle focus plate passes to 0x02013BE0 to draw HP cur/max; paving them blanked every focus-plate HP readout (ISSUE 6). The in-image 12x12 atlas is truly dead in the translated build (its base literal 0x1315C is repointed to the relocated atlas), so the bodies live here, 12 B clear of the 0x11C1FC cave.

cave bodies for the 0x132E2/0x1330E hooks: detect a briefing-region source, load the inline blob pointer, clear the glyph cap, and restore the record cursor on return (shared state word lives just below the cave).
clamps the learn-level list index for the rebuilt getter at 0x98DCA.
re-dispatches control type 6 and un-consumes a >= 0xE0 token high byte for the bark formatter veneer at 0x6588E.

entries.what: parts-caption pad-clip cave (relocated 2026-07-19 off the LIVE unit resource-id table 0x1B1FA8 fam rows — B FAIL#1 処分-detail abort — into the dead in-image atlas)
entries.why: stops the 8px pad-cell loop at the 80px caption strip boundary (caption compose context only); every other context falls through byte-identical.

entries.what: roster navigation cave (relocated 2026-07-19 off the LIVE unit resource-id table 0x1B1FA8 fam rows — B FAIL#1 処分-detail abort — into the dead in-image atlas)
entries.why: cave body for 0x4A66A: edge-detects cursor movement and re-arms the detail/portrait populate state. Its last-cursor-row scratch byte lives at 0x0211C2FD (tail pad of the relocated clamp/populate cave, init 0xFF): the original scratch 0x0233F800 sat INSIDE the stage load buffer [0x0232C800,0x02340000) where _STG98 reaches offset 0x13000 (press-A abort class), and its interim home 0x021B3EFD was vacated when the 0x1B3E22 cave was relocated off the live 'D4'/'/D4' format strings. Its former 12-byte head (the 0x0620F000 admission of the old clip-cave chain) is retired to JP bytes: the map admissions now live complete in the 0x11C448 scoped clip cave.

entries.what: 12x12 trampoline support cave (relocated 2026-07-19 off the LIVE unit resource-id table 0x1B1FA8 fam rows — B FAIL#1 — into the dead in-image atlas)
entries.why: overflow helpers for the 12x12-on-8x16 trampoline (special-cases a few slots and jumps back into the trampoline body).

entries.what: scoped glyph row-stride clip cave (re-homed from PR #3 — its 0x1B3B54 home sits on the LIVE unit resource-id table)
entries.why: one cave now owns the whole clip policy: map 0x06009800 (management/BackStage list) and map 0x0620F000 (info panels) keep the v1.1 whole-map clip; maps 0x0600E000 (MS development system tree) and 0x0600F800 (character/unit Profile lists) are admitted only with the exact context signature origin=(0,0), stride8=13, height=2, style=3 (PR #3's scoping). Matching draws early-return only when (penX+localX)>>3 reaches the row stride; everything else replays the original prologue. 2026-07-20: also admit map 0x0601E000 (in-battle WEAPON-SELECT list) as a whole-map clip — its 12px CJK stat glyphs overran the row stride and wrote past the field into the loaded battle-sprite VRAM (0x06880000), which the scene then reloaded every frame -> infinite load/decompress loop -> hard freeze on units with a wide-enough weapon list (e.g. 精神感应高达Mk2). Appended one ldr/cmp/beq tier-1 check (bne@0x211C460 re-pointed to it) + the 0x0601E000 literal in the same dead-atlas band (0x11C4A4..0x11C4B0); the 4 prior maps and the clamp/no-clamp bodies are byte-identical. Lives in the dead in-image atlas after the 0x11C3F0 cave (band is truly dead: base literal 0x1315C repointed; zero JP word references into 0x11C448..0x11C4A4).

entries.what: 配属 unit-name BG char-tile partition guard
entries.why: the 配属 lower detail panel reserves exactly tile 0x1FF..0x21C for its two-row unit-name bitmap; the next pilot bank begins at 0x21D. A 120px name needs 15 tiles, but the shared copy helper reports r7=18 because it also counts three 8px blank padding tiles, so the 36-tile copy overlaps six pilot-bank tiles and the later pilot draw erases the final unit-name glyphs. This helper matches only the observed unit-name signature (map 0x0620F000, origin=(4,8), stride=32, height=2, style=0xD, tile base 0x1FF) and caps r7 at 15; every other shared-helper context returns unchanged.
clamp body for the second copy-path detour at 0x134AE (info-panel row budget).
clears glyph cells beyond the 6-cell squad name field (called from 0x12CCC); also covers the carried-unit name rows.
if the text style's LUT[3] is transparent, copy LUT[2] into it, then replay the clobbered instructions (called from 0x13C06).

_paved_ref_allowlist.0x23B34: printf arg pool ('共通下：拠点…' @0x021B3F0C, paved by the 0x1B3F0C cave): ldr r0 @0x238A0 feeds only the compiled-out debug printf 0x020A3ECC
_paved_ref_allowlist.0x24438: printf arg pool ('MenuSub_CsrFree1…' @0x021B3FC8, paved by the 0x1B3FC4 cave): ldr r0 @0x240BE -> 0x020A3ECC (compiled-out)
